summary: Backend testing completed for Advanced License Management features. All 13 tests pass after fixing a timezone comparison bug in the renewal endpoint.

fixed.issue: TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes - MongoDB returns naive datetime but code used timezone-aware datetime.now(timezone.utc)
fixed.fix_applied: Added timezone normalization for previous_expiration before comparison

critical_code_review_comments: The timezone handling fix applied is correct but the codebase should be reviewed for similar issues in other datetime comparisons
context_for_next_testing_agent: All advanced license management endpoints are working. Tests use HttpOnly cookie authentication via requests.Session. The known license ID 73120c8f-14c5-4c9c-a7ae-e193d7df8f19 exists and has renewal history.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The renew_license endpoint at line 7014 compared a timezone-naive datetime from MongoDB (previous_expiration) with a timezone-aware datetime (now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)). Python raises TypeError when comparing naive and aware datetimes.
reproduction_steps: 1. Login as super admin with X-Tenant-ID: system header
2. POST /api/licenses/{license_id}/renew with any license that has an expires_at field stored as naive datetime
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Added timezone normalization code to convert naive datetime to UTC-aware before comparison: if previous_expiration.tzinfo is None: previous_expiration = previous_expiration.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)

GET /api/licenses/{id}/history.description: Returns renewal history for a license

PUT /api/licenses/{id}.description: Updates license with new fields (validity_days, salesperson_id, salesperson_name)
